deck: Dimagi puts a stake in the ground: you cannot reach universal health coverage until the jobs of frontline workers get better. Jonathan Jackson and Dr. Neal Lesh on the strategy of improving jobs to improve outcomes.
seo_description: Jonathan Jackson and Dr. Neal Lesh on why Dimagi believes improving frontline worker jobs is essential to better health outcomes and universal health coverage.
og_description: Why Dimagi believes you can't improve health outcomes without first improving frontline worker jobs.

pull_quote.text: Before you can improve outcomes you have to improve the jobs of the people who create those outcomes.

bullets: A stake in the ground: why Dimagi will not consider its vision realized until frontline worker jobs are improved and they are supported to be paid more
From usability to the whole job: how the early focus on making apps less burdensome grew into improving pay, support, and career growth
Jobs vs. productivity: the decision point between making workers more productive and making their jobs genuinely better, and why Dimagi chose the bolder goal
Roots back to 2016: Neal surfaces an old memo where the strategy was already taking shape, long before it was urgent
When digital made jobs worse: vivid examples of redundant reporting tools and growth charts that added burden instead of removing it
Aligned work today: digitizing vaccinator payments across 10+ countries and building resilience skills with health workers
Better jobs, better outcomes: framing improved jobs as synergistic with data, coverage, and other priorities rather than in competition

stats.label: of frontline workers are not fully salaried
countries where Dimagi supports digitized vaccinator payments

guests.name: Dr. Neal Lesh
guests.role: Chief Strategy Officer, central to writing the Improve Jobs to Improve Outcomes strategy and Dimagi's early frontline worker usability work.
